text:
All was quiet at the old cottage by the coast. Sally woke up feeling a little bit dizzy. She blinked twice. Nope, still dizzy. She sat up.

She decided she wanted breakfast. Toast sounded great. She got up.

She didn’t remember falling asleep walking. There was a slice of toast on the dining table.

She sat down at the kitchen table.

No sugar.

She sighed dramatically.

She got up.

She fetched a bowl of cereal.

No milk.

She sighed.

She got up.

She filled a glass.

No water.

She sighed.

She poured cereal into the glass.

No juice.

She sighed deeply.

She drank.

She decided she wanted coffee.

None.

No caffeine.

She sighed.

She considered getting dressed for a walk. Perhaps she’d find something.

Her phone beeped.

It was messages.

Messages? She opened it.

It was her cat.

She laughed.

Dinner?

Outdoors.

By the sea.

She laughed some more.

No apologies needed.
